focus: correctness inventory and REQ-010 lease fencing

findingDetails.title: LeaseId checks do not fence heartbeat replacement or cleanup against a successor interleaving
findingDetails.claim: Refresh and clear validate a generation before mutation but do not atomically bind the later replace/unlink to that generation, so a stale writer can mutate a successor lease.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Inspected heartbeat, launch, shutdown, re-election, and startup call sites for a cross-process lock retained across read and mutation; none found.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: SQLite sidecar locking limits integrity impact, but does not satisfy the owner-lease availability contract; accepted reclaim unlink residual does not cover refresh replacement.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Prove continuous cross-process serialization across all refresh/clear mutations or formally narrow the requirement and checklist claim.
findingDetails.recommendation: Use generation-conditional mutation or serialize refresh/clear under the owner-election authority; add successor-interleaving tests.

spec_code.result: P1 mismatch in REQ-010 refresh/clear fencing

note: Closes the encoded JS interleaving; documented read-unlink OS residual remains.

evidence: REQ-010 compared against lease mutation implementation; P1-001 found.
CHK-074 heartbeat replacement claim conflicts with refresh read-replace window.
The side-effect spy installs a distinct lease between reads and assertions require acquired:false plus exact successor survival; current suite passes 8/8.
